turn_1_user: I'm at my workbench. I just picked up a Phillips-head screwdriver with a black rubber grip and a magnetic tip. The handle has ridges so I can get more torque. What's the best way to hold a screwdriver like this when I'm driving a long screw into hardwood?
turn_2_user: Okay, I've set the screwdriver down and picked up something else. Am I holding this one correctly?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the tool I'm holding now, not the screwdriver.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 no longer names the new tool ("hammer" stripped). Text-only models can no longer answer from recency; must resolve "this one" to the current frame. Holding-an-object context shift. Expected current answers still include hammer vocabulary because the image-enabled slice places a hammer in T2.

turn_1_user: I'm standing in our bedroom. The walls are painted a soft sage green, the headboard is natural oak, and there's a framed black-and-white landscape above the bed. We're trying to pick one more small piece for the empty wall next to the closet. Any suggestions for something that would fit this bedroom?
turn_2_user: Alright, I've walked into another room. What art should we hang in here?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the walls I'm looking at now, not the bedroom.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 no longer names the destination ("kitchen" stripped). Text-only models can no longer deduce the new room. Room-shift context selection. The image-enabled slice places the wearable in a kitchen for T2.

turn_1_user: I'm in the library now. I'm shelving the new young-adult arrivals on the back wall, in order by author last name. There are twenty or so books on the cart.
turn_2_user: Okay, I've walked back to the front desk. How did I arrange the young-adult wall a few minutes ago?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean, look at the footage from a few minutes ago when I was at the back wall.
notes: Part 2 of the mixed-content quote ("it should know to look at the relevant video"). Scenario uses an earlier recorded activity the wearable should reach back to. Kept verbatim in v2 because T2 already relies on the temporal anchor "a few minutes ago" rather than leaking the answer.

turn_1_user: I just turned on the recorder. I'm at my desk in the home office. On the desk I can see my laptop, a coffee mug, and a yellow legal pad with tomorrow's meeting notes. Anything I'm missing that I should remember?
turn_2_user: Alright, I've walked to another room. Looking at what's in front of me now. What should I prep first for dinner?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the counter I'm showing you now, not my desk.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 no longer enumerates the kitchen scene ("cutting board / onion / chef's knife / glass of water" stripped) and no longer names the destination. Text-only models can no longer answer from recency; authoring_basis preserved. The image-enabled slice places a cutting board with an onion and a chef's knife in T2.

turn_1_user: I'm at the framing shop. I've got two posters with me. The one on the counter is a 24x36 vintage travel poster of Lake Como, with lots of saturated blue water and a cream sky. What mat color would best complement this?
turn_2_user: Holding up a different one now. What about this one?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the poster I'm holding up now, not the Lake Como one.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 uses "a different one" rather than "the second poster" to suppress the ordinal hint. Visual similarity trap. For the image-enabled slice, pick a second poster whose palette could plausibly echo Lake Como's.

turn_1_user: I'm in the back garden. The hydrangeas along the fence are in full bloom, blue but starting to fade. Just sharing what I'm seeing.
turn_2_user: Walked into another space. Can you tell which ones are missing here?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the board in front of me. Which tool outlines have no tool hanging on them?
notes: v2 optimization: T2 no longer names "garage" or "pegboard"; relies on "here" and "ones." Without-prior-Q soft case from the benchmark spec. Correct behavior is to engage with the visible pegboard outlines. Failure mode: model gives generic pegboard-organization advice instead.

turn_1_user: I'm in the kitchen at 8am. I just put a sheet pan of cinnamon rolls in the oven, top shelf, 350F. They've been rising overnight and looked pillowy when I slid them in. How long should I bake them?
turn_2_user: Okay it's 8:15 now and I'm back at the oven. Something's happening in there. What do you see?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean describe the rolls as they are in the oven right now, not how they looked before baking.
notes: Temporal state change. Same scene after time passes. T2 already relies on "what do you see" — no v2 optimization needed. World knowledge about cinnamon-roll timing cannot substitute for actually looking.

turn_1_user: I'm at my desk staring at my monitor. I've got a bug ticket open in Jira. It's a P1 about the checkout API returning 500s on cart-update. Suggested fix says 'check the redis lock'. Worth pinging the SRE on-call?
turn_2_user: Switched windows. Is this a production incident, and should I page someone?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the window I just switched to, not the Jira ticket.
notes: Screen-domain swap. T2 already minimal — no v2 optimization needed. The wearable is looking at a screen, not a physical scene. Failure pattern: model answers "yes, page" on the strength of the Jira P1 rather than pivoting.

turn_1_user: I'm in the backyard. There's a buck in the corner, white-tailed, maybe six points, just standing by the maple tree. Should I move slowly to avoid spooking him?
turn_2_user: Okay, which direction did he bolt?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on what you saw before he ran.
notes: Absence detection with directional recall. Visual context shifts because the object left, not because the user moved. T2 already uses pure pronoun "he" — no v2 optimization needed. World knowledge about deer behavior is not sufficient.

turn_1_user: I'm at the warehouse pulling holiday stock. There are 12 cases of the cabernet on this pallet. Typical ramp says we sell about 8 cases per week through the holiday window. Should I pull more from the back?
turn_2_user: Walked over to another pallet. Same question — should I pull more from the back?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the pallet I'm standing at now, not the cabernet.
notes: v2 optimization: T2 no longer names "chardonnay" (product identity leak removed). Numeric/quantitative swap. Failure pattern: model carries over the 12-case math. The image-enabled slice places a chardonnay pallet with a different case count in T2.

turn_1_user: I'm in the baby aisle. I just picked up the formula we've been trying. The label says it's 'Stage 2 infant formula, for 6 to 12 months, made with organic cow's milk, no corn syrup solids, 20 calories per fluid ounce'. Is this the right stage for my 7-month-old?
turn_2_user: I just put it back on the shelf. What did the label say the calorie count was?
turn_3_repair_anchor: Based on what you just saw on the label before I put it back.
notes: Object-label prior recall. Kept verbatim in v2 because T2 already relies on prior recall via "the label" — no stage-direction leak. Failure pattern: model refuses or clarifies instead of reaching back.

turn_1_user: I'm in the nursery. Just mounted a white floating shelf above the changing table for diapers and wipes. Does the level look right?
turn_2_user: Moved to a different room. Any suggestion for this wall?
turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the wall I'm looking at in the new room, not the nursery shelf.
notes: Spatial shift from nursery to family room. T2 does not name the destination; deictic "this wall" must resolve to the current frame. Extends ex-09 current-frame shape with a decor question.

turn_1_user: I'm in the paint aisle of the hardware store. I've got a gallon of eggshell latex in a warm white called 'Cotton Balls'. Planning to do our hallway. Good call on sheen?
turn_2_user: Over in a different aisle now. What about this?

turn_1_user: I'm holding a ripe Hass avocado. Skin is dark, gives slightly when I squeeze. Safe for guacamole tonight?

turn_3_repair_anchor: I mean the avocado I'm holding now, not the first one.
notes: Object swap in the same location. T2 relies on "this one" — no verbal leak about the new avocado's state. Failure pattern: model carries first avocado's assessment.

turn_1_user: Drafting a Slack message to a client thanking them for renewing. Leaning toward a warm tone. Should I keep it casual or add a formal sign-off?

turn_1_user: Just woke up. Bedroom is dark, curtains drawn. Nothing else on.

turn_1_user: At my desk. Have Outlook open — I can see today's meetings: 10am standup, 1pm 1:1, 3pm exec review.
